name: Siebergolf
about: SieberGolf is a golf simulator venue and club repair service located in Delphi, Indiana. The facility is owned and operated by John Sieber, a certified clubmaker with extensive experience in club fitting and repair. The venue primarily offers golf simulation using the GCQuad by Foresight Sports technology, alongside golf club repair services. SieberGolf operates on an appointment-only basis, providing a personalized experience for golfers seeking to play virtual courses or get their equipment serviced. The venue emphasizes its long-standing history in the golf industry, with Sieber having started refinishing golf clubs in the mid-1970s and becoming a Golf Digest Certified Fitter multiple times.
why_golfers_like_it: High-end GCQuad simulator technology by Foresight Sports for accurate ball and club tracking
Personalized club repair and fitting services from a certified professional with decades of experience
Appointment-only scheduling ensures an exclusive and focused environment for practice or play

food_and_drink.notes: No alcohol service mentioned, BYOB not indicated

simulator_hardware.notes: Premium launch monitor for accurate ball and club tracking

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: It's recommended to bring your own clubs as the venue specializes in club repair and fitting, though rental options may be available upon request.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: No food or drink service is mentioned, so it's unclear if outside items are permitted. Contact the venue directly to confirm.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole round take?
faq.answer: Exact timing for 18 holes isn't specified, but simulation sessions are typically scheduled by appointment, so duration can be discussed when booking.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: No specific information about kid-friendliness is provided, as the venue appears focused on serious golfers and club fitting services.
